result.stdout:
Description:
  DynaDocs (dydo) - Documentation-driven context and agent orchestration for AI coding assistants.

Usage:
  dydo [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  check <path>                  Validate documentation and report violations
  fix <path>                    Auto-fix documentation issues where possible
  index <path>                  Regenerate Index.md from doc structure
  init <integration>            Initialize DynaDocs with specified integration
  graph <file>                  Show graph connections for a documentation file
  agent                         Manage agent identity and roles
  guard                         Check if current agent can perform action (used by hooks)
  dispatch                      Dispatch work to another agent
  inbox                         Manage agent inbox
  message, msg                  Send a message to another agent
  wait                          Wait for an incoming message
  task                          Manage tasks
  issue                         Manage issues
  review                        Manage code reviews
  inquisition                   Manage inquisitions
  clean <agent>                 Clean agent workspaces
  workspace                     Manage agent workspace
  whoami                        Show current agent identity
  audit <path>                  View and visualize agent activity logs
  completions <shell>           Output shell completion script
  _complete <position> <words>  Generate shell completions
  template                      Manage templates
  roles                         Manage role definitions
  validate                      Validate dydo configuration, role files, and agent state
  watchdog                      Manage the auto-close watchdog process
  worktree                      Manage git worktrees for agent dispatch
  queue                         Manage dispatch queues
  version                       Display version information
  help                          Display help information

commands.payload:
Description:
  DynaDocs (dydo) - Documentation-driven context and agent orchestration for AI coding assistants.

Usage:
  dydo [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  check <path>                  Validate documentation and report violations
  fix <path>                    Auto-fix documentation issues where possible
  index <path>                  Regenerate Index.md from doc structure
  init <integration>            Initialize DynaDocs with specified integration
  graph <file>                  Show graph connections for a documentation file
  agent                         Manage agent identity and roles
  guard                         Check if current agent can perform action (used by hooks)
  dispatch                      Dispatch work to another agent
  inbox                         Manage agent inbox
  message, msg                  Send a message to another agent
  wait                          Wait for an incoming message
  task                          Manage tasks
  issue                         Manage issues
  review                        Manage code reviews
  inquisition                   Manage inquisitions
  clean <agent>                 Clean agent workspaces
  workspace                     Manage agent workspace
  whoami                        Show current agent identity
  audit <path>                  View and visualize agent activity logs
  completions <shell>           Output shell completion script
  _complete <position> <words>  Generate shell completions
  template                      Manage templates
  roles                         Manage role definitions
  validate                      Validate dydo configuration, role files, and agent state
  watchdog                      Manage the auto-close watchdog process
  worktree                      Manage git worktrees for agent dispatch
  queue                         Manage dispatch queues
  version                       Display version information
  help                          Display help information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage agent identity and roles

Usage:
  dydo agent [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  claim <name>                  Claim an agent for this terminal
  release                       Release the current agent
  status <name>                 Show agent status
  list                          List agents
  role <role>                   Set the current agent's role
  new <name> <human>            Create a new agent and assign to a human
  rename <old-name> <new-name>  Rename an agent
  remove <name>                 Remove an agent from the pool
  reassign <name> <human>       Reassign an agent to a different human
  tree                          Show dispatch hierarchy of active agents

  new <name> <human>            Create a new agent and assign to a human
  rename <old-name> <new-name>  Rename an agent
  remove <name>                 Remove an agent from the pool
  reassign <name> <human>       Reassign an agent to a different human
  tree                          Show dispatch hierarchy of active agents

result.stdout:
Description:
  View and visualize agent activity logs

Usage:
  dydo audit [<path>] [command] [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path filter (e.g., /2025 for year 2025)

Options:
  --list               List available sessions
  --session <session>  Show details for a specific session ID
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

Commands:
  compact <year>  Compact audit snapshots using baseline+delta compression

result.stdout:
Description:
  Dispatch work to another agent

Usage:
  dydo dispatch [options]

Options:
  --role <role> (REQUIRED)   Role for the target agent
  --task <task> (REQUIRED)   Task name
  --brief <brief>            Brief description of the work
  --brief-file <brief-file>  Path to a file containing the brief
  --files <files>            File pattern to include (e.g., 'src/Auth/**')
  --no-launch                Don't launch new terminal, just write to inbox
  --agent, --to <to>         Send dispatch to specific agent (skips auto-selection)
  --escalate                 Mark dispatch as escalated after repeated failures
  --auto-close               Auto-close the dispatched agent's terminal after release
  --tab                      Launch in a new tab (overrides config)
  --new-window               Launch in a new window (overrides config)
  --wait                     Wait for a response from the dispatched agent
  --no-wait                  Dispatch and return immediately (fire and forget)
  --worktree                 Run dispatched agent in a git worktree for isolated work
  --queue <queue>            Named queue to serialize terminal launch (e.g., --queue merge)
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Dispatch work to another agent

Usage:
  dydo dispatch [options]

Options:
  --role <role> (REQUIRED)   Role for the target agent
  --task <task> (REQUIRED)   Task name
  --brief <brief>            Brief description of the work
  --brief-file <brief-file>  Path to a file containing the brief
  --files <files>            File pattern to include (e.g., 'src/Auth/**')
  --no-launch                Don't launch new terminal, just write to inbox
  --agent, --to <to>         Send dispatch to specific agent (skips auto-selection)
  --escalate                 Mark dispatch as escalated after repeated failures
  --auto-close               Auto-close the dispatched agent's terminal after release
  --tab                      Launch in a new tab (overrides config)
  --new-window               Launch in a new window (overrides config)
  --wait                     Wait for a response from the dispatched agent
  --no-wait                  Dispatch and return immediately (fire and forget)
  --worktree                 Run dispatched agent in a git worktree for isolated work
  --queue <queue>            Named queue to serialize terminal launch (e.g., --queue merge)
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show graph connections for a documentation file

Usage:
  dydo graph <file> [command] [options]

Arguments:
  <file>  The doc file to analyze

Options:
  --incoming         Show docs that link TO this file (backlinks)
  --degree <degree>  Show docs within n link-hops (default: 1) [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

Commands:
  stats  Show document link statistics ranked by incoming links

result.stdout:
Description:
  Check if current agent can perform action (used by hooks)

Usage:
  dydo guard [command] [options]

Options:
  --action <action>    Action being attempted (edit, write, delete, read)
  --path <path>        Path being accessed
  --command <command>  Bash command to analyze
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

Commands:
  lift <agent> <minutes>  Temporarily lift RBAC restrictions for an agent
  restore <agent>         Restore RBAC restrictions for an agent

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a new issue

Usage:
  dydo issue create [options]

Options:
  --title <title> (REQUIRED)        Issue title
  --area <area> (REQUIRED)          Issue area (e.g., backend, frontend, general)
  --severity <severity> (REQUIRED)  Issue severity (low, medium, high, critical)
  --found-by <found-by>             How the issue was found (inquisition, review, manual). Defaults to manual.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Send a message to another agent

Options:
  --to <to> (REQUIRED)     Target agent name
  --body <body>            Message content
  --body-file <body-file>  Read body from file
  --subject <subject>      Topic/task identifier
  --force                  Allow sending to inactive agents
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

  --force                  Allow sending to inactive agents
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage git worktrees for agent dispatch

Usage:
  dydo worktree [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  cleanup <worktree-id>  Remove agent's worktree markers and clean up worktree if last reference
  merge                  Merge worktree branch back into base branch
  init-settings          Copy settings.local.json to worktree with Read permission for main repo path
  prune                  Remove orphaned worktree directories and stale markers
